**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the system-level storefront deconstruction and the '10k RMB capital electromechanical broker/scrap collector' physical demotion probe. This enterprise, named 'Lifeng Electromechanical Technology,' was hastily categorized into 'Electrical Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing' by the official big data system simply because its name includes 'Electromechanical Technology' and its business license begins with 'development of electromechanical products.' However, the forensic logic engine directly extracts the most laughable baseline data of the entire batch: its paid-in capital is a tragic 10,000 RMB! The total insured headcount of the company is exactly 1 person! The logical deduction instantly turns into a slice-of-life documentary: in a small ground-floor storefront or cubicle (4-109) in the Rhine Fortune Plaza, the owner (the sole insured person) clutches 10,000 RMB in capital and claims to have built an 'Electrical Machinery Manufacturing Plant'? The probe mercilessly deletes the facade word 'development' from the license and extracts what is actually done with 10,000 RMB: 'air conditioning sales and installation; computer maintenance; auto repair; recycling of waste materials (scrap collection); sales of handicrafts and primary agricultural products.' The truth is extremely naked and somewhat poignant: this is a 100% pure 1-Employee Appliance Repair & Scrap Recycling Micro-Shop operating on a 10,000 RMB shell. The owner is a typical 'universal repairman,' primarily making a living by installing air conditioners, fixing computers, and repairing cars. If they encounter unwanted old appliances, they collect them as scrap (recycling of waste materials); occasionally, they might also sell some radishes and cabbages (primary agricultural products) or small ornaments (handicrafts) in the shop. The official 'Electrical Manufacturing Entity' tag is the most outrageous system-level mockery of China's most grassroots street-level repair stalls.
